known_biases: effective_n (cross-sectional cluster, ~266) is recorded as metadata only; p-values / DSR use time-series n_periods (no automatic cross-sectional shrinkage applied)
bootstrap CI uses Politis-Romano stationary block resampling to preserve autocorrelation
permutation null uses per (iter, period) independent seed
DSR uses empirical period-IC moments (skew=0.279, kurtosis=2.310)
universe drawn from local cache scan; delisted symbols absent (survivorship bias)
forward return is price-only (no dividend adjustment); total-return upgrade deferred to Phase A2

fixes_applied: P1-A: as-of issued_capital lookup helper (panel + asof signature)
R28-1 follow-up: ran cache_fill_new_factors.py --seed-issued-capital to populate issued_capital cache with date column (157374 rows, 1952 unique symbols)

IMPORTANT CAVEAT (R28-1 empirical): seed_issued_capital derives shares = market_value / close, but mv cache uses latest_shares × historical_close (per finmind.py:1033) → derived shares are static across all dates per symbol. Effective behavior identical to fallback Timestamp.min static-snapshot.
Quantified: R27 fallback Timestamp.min IC = +0.0387; R28 with new panel IC = +0.0388 (Δ +0.0001). Confirms derive method is form-correct but substance-equivalent to static snapshot.
TRUE PIT requires new TWSE OpenAPI scraper for historical issued_shares snapshots (P1 backlog, not in current plan scope).
pit_violation.r28_followup_finding: Form-correct but no substance change; margin_short IC = +0.0388 still uses latest issued_shares as denominator (static-snapshot approximation).
